Description: Establish the prerequisites, access and deployment decision needed before installing.
Install and run the tracker on a workstation or host.
Configure environment variables and paths for the target environment.
Move existing tracker data into the application database and validate it.
Deploy a single shared instance for the team (Model B only).
Set up the optional job-hunt automation that feeds the tracker.
Verify the install works end to end and gain team sign-off.
Document operations and hand over to the team.
Check Python 3.9+ and git are available on each target machine or host. AC: 1) python3 --version is 3.9 or higher; 2) git installed; 3) result recorded per machine.

Summary: Grant repository access to the team
Description: Add each team member as a collaborator on the private repo. AC: 1) every member can clone the repo; 2) access list reviewed.

Summary: Decide and record the deployment model
Description: Choose Model A (local per person) or Model B (shared instance) and document it. AC: 1) model chosen with rationale; 2) decision recorded; 3) dependent stories enabled or disabled.

Summary: Provision host or VM for shared instance
Description: Model B only. Provision a Linux host or container platform reachable by the team. AC: 1) host reachable; 2) Docker or Python runtime available; 3) network/VPN access confirmed.

Summary: Clone repository and create virtual environment
Description: Clone the repo and create a Python venv. AC: 1) repo cloned; 2) .venv created and activated.
Install runtime dependencies. AC: 1) pip install -r requirements.txt completes with no errors.

Summary: Launch the app and verify it loads
Description: Run Streamlit and confirm the UI loads. AC: 1) streamlit run app.py starts; 2) localhost:8501 shows the tracker.

Summary: Load demo data and validate the UI
Description: Seed demo rows and check the core UI. AC: 1) python seed.py loads sample rows; 2) metrics, table and filters render; 3) demo data cleared before real use.
Create a local .env file. AC: 1) .env copied from .env.example; 2) confirmed git-ignored.
Set JOBTRACKER_DB to the intended path. AC: 1) app reads and writes the configured database path.
Optional. Point JOBTRACKER_IMPORT_DB at the external database. AC: 1) the Import new roles button finds the source.

Summary: Inventory and back up existing tracker data
Description: Locate the current spreadsheet or data and back it up. AC: 1) source located; 2) backup stored safely; 3) row count noted.
Import the spreadsheet into jobs.db. AC: 1) migrate.py completes; 2) import summary printed.
Validate counts and spot-check records. AC: 1) imported count matches expected unique roles; 2) at least 5 records spot-checked against source; 3) links and statuses correct.
Model B. Build a container image. AC: 1) Dockerfile builds; 2) container serves the app on 8501.

Summary: Deploy the instance with persistent storage
Description: Model B. Run the instance with a persistent volume for jobs.db. AC: 1) app reachable by the team; 2) jobs.db persists across restarts.

Summary: Add access control in front of the app
Description: Model B. Streamlit has no built-in auth; restrict access. AC: 1) only authorized users can reach the app; 2) method documented.
Model B. Schedule regular jobs.db backups. AC: 1) scheduled backup runs; 2) a restore has been tested.

Summary: Install and connect the Claude in Chrome extension
Description: Optional automation. Install and connect the browser extension. AC: 1) extension installed; 2) connection confirmed.

Summary: Generate the job-hunt skill from the prompt
Description: Use examples/generate-job-hunt-skill.md to build the skill. AC: 1) placeholders filled with real criteria; 2) skill created and runnable.

Summary: Wire the skill output into the tracker
Description: Connect the skill database to the tracker. AC: 1) JOBTRACKER_IMPORT_DB set or DB shared; 2) import works.

Summary: Run an end-to-end job-hunt and import test
Description: Run a real sweep and import. AC: 1) skill finds and logs roles; 2) import brings them in with no duplicates.
Run pytest and ruff. AC: 1) all tests pass; 2) ruff check is clean.
Exercise the main user flows. AC: 1) add/edit/delete/save works; 2) filters work; 3) CSV export works.
Confirm WAL concurrency. AC: 1) a write succeeds while the app is open elsewhere; 2) no database is locked errors.
Team validates against real tasks. AC: 1) representative users complete their workflow; 2) sign-off recorded.
Produce a concise team quickstart or runbook. AC: 1) covers start/stop, add/edit, import, export; 2) reviewed by a non-author.
Document and test backup and restore. AC: 1) procedure written; 2) a restore validated.
Train the team on the tracker. AC: 1) session delivered; 2) questions captured.
Assign an owner and a support route. AC: 1) named owner; 2) support channel agreed.
